resources.string: Sweep peaks at 80% of this, capped to the EL15\'s 12 A / 150 W.

EL15 Load Control — an Android client for the ALIENTEK EL15 electronic load over Bluetooth Low Energy.

Version %1$s

BLE protocol reverse-engineered from the DM40GUI desktop project (github.com/maj113/DM40GUI). Includes live monitoring, a waveform recorder with CSV export, and a fuse-aware circuit-resistance test with a detailed, exportable report.

This is an independent client and is not affiliated with ALIENTEK.
Loads the circuit in steps up to 80% of the fuse rating, then computes resistance from the V–I slope.
This will draw up to %1$.2f A — limited by %2$s. The sweep stays within the EL15\'s 12 A / 150 W ratings. Make sure your wiring and source can handle it, then keep clear.
Metrics per step — V, I, P, temp, fan (normalized)
Choose what appears in the printed / shared report.
No saved tests yet.\nEvery completed resistance test is archived here automatically.
Delete the archived test (%1$s)? This cannot be undone.
⚠ Low-confidence fit — voltage barely changed under load. Check connections, or use a lower-impedance path / higher current.

Calibration probes the connected circuit to find a good balance of accuracy, speed and detail:

1. Measures how long readings take to settle after a load step
2. Measures the voltage noise floor to size the averaging window
3. Runs short sweeps (5 → 8 → 12 steps) until the measured resistance converges

It will briefly draw up to %1$.2f A, takes roughly %2$s, and always switches the load off when done.

How many current levels the sweep climbs through on its way to the peak current (up to 1000).\n\nEach step contributes one averaged data point to the V–I fit, the graphs, and the table — more steps give a tighter resistance fit and finer curves, at the cost of a longer test. The estimated duration is shown when the test starts.

How long the load holds each new current level before sampling begins, giving the circuit time to stabilise after the setpoint change.\n\nAny value is accepted. Very short values are automatically stretched to just past the poll interval so at least one fresh reading arrives after settling.

How long readings are collected and averaged at each step, after settling. Longer windows smooth out noise for a cleaner fit.\n\nAny value is accepted. Very short values are automatically stretched to about two poll intervals so every step captures at least two readings.
🧪 EL15 Demo (Simulator)\nTap to set up a virtual circuit
Demo — %1$.1f V, %2$.3f Ω · tap to edit circuit
Set the virtual circuit the demo load sees. A resistance test will recover these values. Try a higher voltage (e.g. 48 V) to see the sweep power-limit to the EL15\'s 150 W.
